zh.Weston Favell, Northamptonshire, England: 英國諾薩普頓史爾韋斯頓 法維爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr於1326年11月30日出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾韋斯頓 法維爾時，他的父親John de la Warre32歲，母親Margaret de Holand18歲。他於1338年10月14日之前與Elizabeth De Welle在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1370年8月27日在法國享年43歲，埋葬在英國林可爾史爾斯文黑阿德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr于1326年11月30日出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾韋斯頓 法維爾时，他的父亲John de la Warre32岁，母亲Margaret de Holand18岁。他于1338年10月14日之前与Elizabeth De Welle在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1370年8月27日在法国享年43岁，埋葬在英国林可爾史爾斯文黑阿德。

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Warre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Warr
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname for a warrior, usually from Norman French were, werre, waire, warre (central French guerre) ‘war, battle’, and short for de la werre (de la guerre, see Delaware ), literally ‘(man) of the war’, a construction paralleled in de la Bataille (see Battle ). Occasionally it is a shortened form of Old French werreur ‘warrior’.

variants.name: Ware
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by or looked after a dam or weir on a river (from Old English wær, wer, Middle English war(e), warre, wer(e), werre ‘weir’), or a habitational name from a place called with this word, such as Weare (Somerset), Weare Giffard (Devon), or Ware (Hertfordshire).
topographic name for someone who lived by a horse pond or fish pond, from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English wayour, which became early modern English ware, wear.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr was born on 30 November 1326, in Weston Favell, Northamptonshire, England, his father, John de la Warre, was 32 and his mother, Margaret de Holand, was 18. He married Elizabeth De Welle before 14 October 1338, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 27 August 1370, in France, at the age of 43, and was buried in Swineshead, Lincolnshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr est né le 30 novembre 1326, à Weston Favell, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, John de la Warre, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Holand, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth De Welle avant 14 octobre 1338, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 27 août 1370, à France, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Swineshead, Lincolnshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr am 30. November 1326 in Weston Favell, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de la Warre 32 und seine Mutter Margaret de Holand 18. Er hat vor 14. Oktober 1338 in England, United Kingdom Elizabeth De Welle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 27. August 1370 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Frankreich und wurde in Swineshead, Lincolnshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr è nato il il 30 novembre 1326 nel Weston Favell, Northamptonshire, England, il suo padre, John de la Warre, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Margaret de Holand, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth De Welle prima del 14 ottobre 1338, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 27 agosto 1370, nel Francia, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolto a Swineshead, Lincolnshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warrが1326年11月30日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアウエストン ファベルで生まれた時，父John de la Warreは32歳，母Margaret de Holandは18歳であった。1338年10月14日以前にイギリスイギリスでElizabeth De Welleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1370年8月27日にフランスで43歳で死去し，イギリスリンコルンシュアイアスウィンエシェアドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr(이)가 1326년 11월 30일 영국 노르섬프토실르 웨스턴 파벨에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de la Warre(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret de Holand(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1338년 10월 14일 이전 영국 잉글랜드에서 Elizabeth De Welle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1370년 8월 27일 프랑스에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 링코실르 스윈셰아드에 묻혔다.

pt.30 November 1326–27 August 1370: 30 de novembro de 1326–27 de agosto de 1370
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr nasceu em 30 de novembro de 1326, em Weston Favell, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de la Warre, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Holand, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth De Welle antes 14 de outubro de 1338, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 27 de agosto de 1370, em França, com 43 anos, e foi sepultado em Swineshead, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr родился 30 ноября 1326 г. в местности вестон фавелл, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени John de la Warre было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Holand, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth De Welle перед 14 октябрь 1338 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 27 августа 1370 г. в местности Франция в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности сваинешид, линколншир, Англия.

es.30 November 1326–27 August 1370: 30 de noviembre de 1326–27 de agosto de 1370
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Roger la Warre 3rd Baron De La Warr nació el 30 de noviembre de 1326, en Weston Favell, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John de la Warre, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Margaret de Holand, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Elizabeth De Welle antes de 14 de octubre de 1338, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 27 de agosto de 1370, en Francia, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Swineshead, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.
